emission_adjustment.description: The Government adjusts the carbon tax rate based on the ratio of aggregate emissions (total_emission) relative to the emissions target (emission_target). If emissions are significantly higher than the target, the tax increases; if significantly lower, the tax decreases.

conditions.explanation: Aggregate emissions exceeding the target by more than the upper threshold ratio trigger a tax rate increase to incentivize better performance.
Aggregate emissions falling below the target by more than the lower threshold ratio trigger a tax rate decrease as a reward for strong performance.

market_alignment.description: The tax rate is partially adjusted towards the external carbon market price when the market price exceeds a significant multiple of the statutory minimum rate or falls below the minimum rate. The minimum tax rate acts as a floor for the carbon tax.

conditions.explanation: When the market price significantly exceeds the statutory minimum, a partial tax increase towards the market price is triggered to maintain consistency.
When the market price falls below the statutory minimum tax rate (Rp30.00/kg CO₂e), the tax is maintained at the minimum rate, preventing a full downward alignment.
